name: AgentLifecycleRegistry
description:
Optional registry that collects per-instance agent lifecycles within
an Angular injection context. External instrumentation packages
(e.g. cockpit-telemetry) provide this token and read from it.

`@threadplane/langgraph` does NOT provide this itself — `agent()` writes to
the registry only when an external consumer has provided it.

properties.name: lifecycles
properties.description: Reactive list of registered lifecycles.

name: FakeStreamTransport
description:
In-process AgentTransport that auto-streams a canned assistant reply.

Backs `provideFakeAgent()`. Unlike `MockAgentTransport` (passive, driven
manually from specs), this transport emits its tokens automatically on
`stream()`, then completes — suitable for offline demos and integration tests.

NOT for production use.

methods.name: cancelRun
methods.description: Optional: cancel a server-side run.

methods.name: createQueuedRun
methods.signature: createQueuedRun(_assistantId: string, threadId: string, payload: unknown, _signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): Promise<AgentQueueEntry<unknown>>
methods.description: Optional: create a server-side queued run without joining it immediately.

methods.name: getHistory
methods.description: Optional: load persisted checkpoint history for a thread.

methods.name: joinStream
methods.description: Optional: join an already-started run without creating a new one.

methods.name: stream
methods.signature: stream(_assistantId: string, _threadId: string | null, _payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, _options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Open a streaming connection to an agent and yield events.

name: FetchStreamTransport
description:
Production transport that connects to a LangGraph Platform API via HTTP and SSE.

Creates threads automatically if no threadId is provided, and streams events
using the LangGraph SDK client.

params.name: apiUrl
params.description: Base URL of the LangGraph Platform API

params.name: onThreadId
params.description: Optional callback invoked when a new thread is created

methods.name: cancelRun
methods.signature: cancelRun(threadId: string, runId: string, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<void>
methods.description: Cancel a server-side run.

methods.name: createQueuedRun
methods.signature: createQueuedRun(assistantId: string, threadId: string, payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): Promise<AgentQueueEntry<unknown>>
methods.description: Create a pending server-side run using LangGraph's enqueue strategy.

methods.name: getHistory
methods.description: Load persisted checkpoint history for a thread.

methods.name: joinStream
methods.signature: joinStream(threadId: string, runId: string, lastEventId: string | undefined, signal: AbortSignal): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Join an already-started run without creating a new thread.

methods.name: stream
methods.signature: stream(assistantId: string, threadId: string | null, payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Open a streaming connection, creating a thread if needed.

methods.name: updateState
methods.signature: updateState(threadId: string, values: Record<string, unknown>, _signal: AbortSignal, options: object): Promise<void>
methods.description: Update server-side thread state, e.g. to remove messages for regenerate rollback.

name: LangGraphThreadsAdapter
description:
SDK-backed thread store. Wraps `client.threads.*` and maps SDK
threads to the framework's Thread type for direct use with
`<chat-thread-list>` / `<chat-sidenav>`.

Consumers wire the framework's `ThreadActionAdapter` to instance
methods (rename/delete/archive/pin/...) so the right-click menu
round-trips through the LangGraph SDK without per-app boilerplate.

properties.name: archivedThreads
properties.description: Threads whose `metadata.archived === true`.

properties.name: threads
properties.description: Active (non-archived) threads, sorted with pinned first.

methods.name: getThread
methods.description:
Fetch a single thread by id. Returns `null` when the server
 returns 404 (thread doesn't exist) so callers can distinguish
 "missing" from "couldn't reach the server" — genuine network
 errors rethrow. Used by URL-based thread routing to validate a
 pasted/shared thread id before activating it.

methods.name: moveToProject
methods.signature: moveToProject(threadId: string, projectId: string | null): Promise<void>

methods.name: refresh
methods.description:
Fetch the latest thread list from the server. Failures are
 logged via `console.error` (not swallowed silently — silent
 catches have masked prod issues in the past).

 Invocation and resolution are logged at `console.debug` so prod
 inspection can distinguish "never called" from "called but
 resolved empty" from "called and threw." This was prompted by a
 demo.threadplane.ai cold-load bug where the sidenav stayed empty
 with no visible signal. Tighten the log volume if it becomes
 noisy.

methods.name: reorderPinned
methods.signature: reorderPinned(threadId: string, beforeId: string | null): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Re-stamp `metadata.pinnedOrder = 0,1,2,...` for the pinned slice
 to reflect the new ordering.

name: MockAgentTransport
description:
Test transport for deterministic agent testing without a real LangGraph server.

Script event batches upfront, then emit them manually or step through them
in your test specs. Supports error injection and close control.

params.name: script
params.description: Array of event batches. Each batch is emitted as a group.

methods.name: cancelRun
methods.signature: cancelRun(threadId: string, runId: string, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<void>
methods.description: Optional: cancel a server-side run.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Close the stream. Remaining queued events are drained before completion.

methods.name: createQueuedRun
methods.signature: createQueuedRun(_assistantId: string, threadId: string, payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): Promise<AgentQueueEntry<unknown>>
methods.description: Optional: create a server-side queued run without joining it immediately.

methods.name: emit
methods.description: Manually emit events into the stream.

methods.name: emitError
methods.description: Inject an error into the stream.

methods.name: getHistory
methods.description: Optional: load persisted checkpoint history for a thread.

methods.name: isStreaming
methods.description: Returns true if a stream is currently active.

methods.name: joinStream
methods.signature: joinStream(threadId: string, runId: string, lastEventId: string | undefined, signal: AbortSignal): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Optional: join an already-started run without creating a new one.

methods.name: nextBatch
methods.description: Advance to the next scripted batch. Pass the returned events to `emit()`.

methods.name: stream
methods.signature: stream(_assistantId: string, _threadId: string | null, _payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Open a streaming connection to an agent and yield events.

name: AgentBranchTree
description: Tree representation of LangGraph checkpoint history for time-travel UIs.

name: AgentBranchTreeFork
description: A branch fork where each item is an alternate checkpoint sequence.

name: AgentBranchTreeNode
description: A checkpoint entry in the experimental branch tree.

name: AgentConfig
description:
Configuration for an agent instance.
Combines connection defaults with per-component options so the
agent can be constructed once at provider time and injected
everywhere it is needed.

properties.name: apiUrl
properties.description: Base URL of the LangGraph Platform API (e.g., `'http://localhost:2024'`).

properties.name: assistantId
properties.description: Agent or graph identifier on the LangGraph platform.

properties.name: clientOptions
properties.description: Tuning options for the default transport's LangGraph SDK client (e.g. retry budget).

properties.name: filterSubagentMessages
properties.description: When true, subagent messages are filtered from the main messages signal.

properties.name: initialValues
properties.description: Initial state values before the first stream response arrives.

properties.name: onThreadId
properties.description: Called when a new thread is auto-created by the transport.

properties.name: subagentToolNames
properties.description: Tool names that indicate a subagent invocation.

properties.name: telemetry
properties.description: Optional app-owned telemetry sink. No telemetry is emitted unless this is provided.

properties.name: threadId
properties.description: Thread ID to connect to. Pass a Signal for reactive thread switching.

properties.name: throttle
properties.description: Throttle signal updates in milliseconds. `false` to disable.

properties.name: toMessage
properties.description: Custom message deserializer for non-standard message formats.

properties.name: transport
properties.description: Custom transport. Defaults to FetchStreamTransport.

properties.name: interruptReceivedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms of the first interrupt$ non-null in this stream. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: interruptResolvedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms of the most recent submit({ resume }) call. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: streamErrorAt
properties.description: Epoch ms + classification of the most recent stream error. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: streamStartedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms of the first stream chunk arrival. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: threadCreatedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms when the agent's "create new thread" branch fired. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: threadPersistedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms when an existing thread was restored from server (proves persistence). Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: toolCallCompletedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms of the first tool call result transition. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: toolCallStartedAt
properties.description: Epoch ms of the first tool call append. Resets on switchThread().

properties.name: apiUrl
properties.description: Base URL of the LangGraph Platform API. Defaults to `provideAgent({ apiUrl })` when omitted.

properties.name: assistantId
properties.description: Agent or graph identifier on the LangGraph platform.

properties.name: clientOptions
properties.description: Tuning options for the default transport's LangGraph SDK client (e.g. retry budget).

properties.name: filterSubagentMessages
properties.description: When true, subagent messages are filtered from the main messages signal.

properties.name: initialValues
properties.description: Initial state values before the first stream response arrives.

properties.name: onThreadId
properties.description: Called when a new thread is auto-created by the transport.

properties.name: subagentToolNames
properties.description: Tool names that indicate a subagent invocation.

properties.name: telemetry
properties.description: Optional app-owned telemetry sink. No telemetry is emitted unless this is provided.

properties.name: threadId
properties.description: Thread ID to connect to. Pass a Signal for reactive thread switching.

properties.name: throttle
properties.description: Throttle signal updates in milliseconds. `false` to disable.

properties.name: toMessage
properties.description: Custom message deserializer for non-standard message formats.

properties.name: transport
properties.description: Custom transport. Defaults to FetchStreamTransport.

name: AgentQueue
description: Public queue surface for pending server-side LangGraph runs.

properties.name: cancel
properties.description: Cancel a specific pending run by server run ID.

properties.name: clear
properties.description: Cancel all pending runs and clear the queue.

properties.name: entries
properties.description: Read-only pending queue entries.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Number of pending queue entries.

name: AgentQueueEntry
description: A queued server-side LangGraph run.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: Timestamp when the queued run was registered locally.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Server-side run ID.

properties.name: options
properties.description: Submit options used when the queued run was created.

properties.name: threadId
properties.description: Thread that owns the queued run.

properties.name: values
properties.description: Values submitted for the queued run.

name: AgentTransport
description: Transport interface for connecting to a LangGraph agent.

methods.name: cancelRun
methods.signature: cancelRun(threadId: string, runId: string, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<void>
methods.description: Optional: cancel a server-side run.

methods.name: createQueuedRun
methods.signature: createQueuedRun(assistantId: string, threadId: string, payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): Promise<AgentQueueEntry<unknown>>
methods.description: Optional: create a server-side queued run without joining it immediately.

methods.name: getHistory
methods.description: Optional: load persisted checkpoint history for a thread.

methods.name: joinStream
methods.signature: joinStream(threadId: string, runId: string, lastEventId: string | undefined, signal: AbortSignal): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Optional: join an already-started run without creating a new one.

methods.name: stream
methods.signature: stream(assistantId: string, threadId: string | null, payload: unknown, signal: AbortSignal, options: LangGraphSubmitOptions): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>
methods.description: Open a streaming connection to an agent and yield events.

methods.name: updateState
methods.signature: updateState(threadId: string, values: Record<string, unknown>, signal: AbortSignal, options: object): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Optional: update server-side thread state (e.g. to emit RemoveMessage
entries for regenerate rollback). Forwards to the LangGraph
`threads.updateState` API.

`options.asNode` corresponds to LangGraph's `as_node` parameter — the
server treats the update as if that node had just produced the values,
which determines what the next pull resumes. `regenerate()` passes
`asNode: '__start__'` so the next `submit(null)` resumes at the entry
node and re-runs `generate` against the rolled-back state.

name: CustomStreamEvent
description: A custom event emitted by the LangGraph backend via adispatch_custom_event().

properties.name: data
properties.description: Arbitrary payload from the backend.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Event name set by the backend (e.g., 'state_update').

name: Interrupt
description: An interrupt thrown inside a thread.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The ID of the interrupt.

properties.name: ns
properties.description: The namespace of the interrupt.

properties.name: resumable
properties.description: Whether the interrupt can be resumed.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value of the interrupt.

properties.name: when
properties.description: Will be deprecated in the future.

name: LangGraphAgent
description:
Unified LangGraph agent surface returned by `injectAgent()`.

Extends the runtime-neutral `AgentWithHistory` contract (chat-consumable)
with the full LangGraph-specific API. One object drives both `<chat>` and
any LangGraph-specific demo. Raw LangGraph signals are prefixed with
`langGraph` to avoid collision with the runtime-neutral names.

properties.name: activeSubagents
properties.description: Filtered list of subagents with status 'running'.

properties.name: branch
properties.description: Current branch identifier for time-travel navigation.

properties.name: clientTools
properties.description:
Client-declared, client-executed tools. Call setCatalog() to register
tool specs; the catalog is automatically shipped with every run via
`input.client_tools`. Pending tool calls appear in pending() after
the run ends; resolve() returns a result and continues the run.

properties.name: customEvents
properties.description:
Raw custom events stream (signal of array). The runtime-neutral
 `events$` Observable is derived from this.

properties.name: experimentalBranchTree
properties.description: Experimental branch tree derived from LangGraph checkpoint history.

properties.name: getMessagesMetadata
properties.type: (msg: BaseMessage<>, idx: number) => MessageMetadata<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined
properties.description: Get metadata for a specific message by index.

properties.name: getSubagent
properties.description: Get a subagent stream by the tool call ID that spawned it.

properties.name: getSubagentsByMessage
properties.description: Get subagent streams spawned by the tool calls on a specific AI message.

properties.name: getSubagentsByType
properties.description: Get subagent streams by their configured subagent type/name.

properties.name: getToolCalls
properties.description: Get tool call results associated with an AI message (LangGraph types).

properties.name: hasValue
properties.description: True once at least one value or message has been received.

properties.name: isThreadLoading
properties.description: True while a thread switch is loading state from the server.

properties.name: joinStream
properties.description: Join an already-running stream by run ID.

properties.name: langGraphHistory
properties.description: Raw LangGraph history (ThreadState[]). Use `history` for AgentCheckpoint[].

properties.name: langGraphInterrupts
properties.description: All interrupts received during the current run (raw LangGraph shape).

properties.name: langGraphMessages
properties.description: Raw LangChain BaseMessage list. Use `messages` for chat rendering.

properties.name: langGraphToolCalls
properties.description: Raw LangGraph tool calls (with run-state). Use `toolCalls` for chat rendering.

properties.name: lifecycle
properties.description:
Lifecycle signals for observability/telemetry. Eight read-only signals
capture key transitions (first stream chunk, first interrupt, tool
call start/complete, thread create/persist, errors). All reset on
`switchThread()`. See AgentLifecycle.

properties.name: messageCheckpoints
properties.description:
Optional reactive map of `messageId → checkpointId`, computed by
walking history once: for each checkpoint, find the most recent
assistant message present in its `values.messages` and pair them.
UIs use this to anchor inline checkpoint markers on each assistant
turn. Missing on adapters that don't compute it.

properties.name: queue
properties.description: Pending server-side runs created via `multitaskStrategy: 'enqueue'`.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description:
Discards the assistant message at the given index AND all messages after
it, then re-runs the agent against the trimmed conversation tail. The
preceding user message (at index - 1) is preserved and re-submitted as
the agent's input. No new user message is added to the history.

Throws if the message at `index` is not 'assistant' role, or if the
agent is currently loading another response.

properties.name: reload
properties.description: Re-submit the last input to restart the stream.

properties.name: setBranch
properties.description: Set the active branch for time-travel navigation.

properties.name: submit
properties.type: (input: AgentSubmitInput | null | undefined, opts: AgentSubmitOptions & LangGraphSubmitOptions) => Promise<void>
properties.description: Submit input, resume commands, checkpoint forks, or other LangGraph run options.

properties.name: switchThread
properties.description: Switch to a different thread, resetting derived state.

properties.name: toolProgress
properties.description: Progress updates for currently executing tools.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Current agent state values (raw, typed per the type parameter T).

name: LangGraphClientOptions
description:
Tuning options for the underlying LangGraph SDK `Client` constructed by the
default FetchStreamTransport. Ignored when a custom `transport` is
supplied (the transport owns its own client).

properties.name: maxRetries
properties.description:
How many times a failed request — including the initial stream connect —
is retried with exponential backoff before the error surfaces. Maps to the
SDK's `callerOptions.maxRetries`. Omitted → the SDK default (currently 4).

Set `0` to fail fast: useful for e2e tests that force a connection failure
and assert the error surfaces promptly, rather than after the full
multi-second backoff window.

name: LangGraphSubmitOptions
description: Options accepted by LangGraph-backed submit calls.

properties.name: multitaskStrategy
properties.description: Strategy for handling concurrent runs on the same thread.

properties.name: resume
properties.description: Convenience alias normalized to `command.resume` before invoking LangGraph.

name: LangGraphThreadsConfig
description:
Configuration consumed by LangGraphThreadsAdapter. Provide
via LANGGRAPH_THREADS_CONFIG (typically in app.config.ts):

```ts
providers: [
  { provide: LANGGRAPH_THREADS_CONFIG, useValue: {
      apiUrl: environment.langGraphApiUrl,
  }},
],
```

The adapter expects backends to write the thread title to
`metadata.title`. Spec 2026-05-19-llm-generated-labels-design.md
originally proposed `metadata.thread_title` for cockpit caps but
we converged on `title` to match the canonical demo and avoid a
per-cap configuration knob.

properties.name: apiUrl
properties.description:
Base URL for the LangGraph Platform API. Accepts both absolute
 URLs and relative `/api`-style paths.

properties.name: titleFallback
properties.description:
Fallback label for threads whose title hasn't been written yet
 (e.g. created but never sent). Defaults to `'Untitled'`.

name: MockLangGraphAgent
description:
A LangGraphAgent mock with writable signals for easy test control.

Builds on the runtime-neutral MockAgent from `@threadplane/chat`
(which supplies the neutral `Agent`-contract writable signals plus
`submit`/`stop`/`regenerate` call tracking) and layers the LangGraph-specific
writable signals on top.

Cast the result of `mockLangGraphAgent()` to this type to access
writable signals without unsafe casts in test files.

properties.name: activeSubagents
properties.description: Filtered list of subagents with status 'running'.

properties.name: branch
properties.description: Current branch identifier for time-travel navigation.

properties.name: clientTools
properties.description:
Client-declared, client-executed tools. Call setCatalog() to register
tool specs; the catalog is automatically shipped with every run via
`input.client_tools`. Pending tool calls appear in pending() after
the run ends; resolve() returns a result and continues the run.

properties.name: customEvents
properties.description:
Raw custom events stream (signal of array). The runtime-neutral
 `events$` Observable is derived from this.

properties.name: experimentalBranchTree
properties.description: Experimental branch tree derived from LangGraph checkpoint history.

properties.name: getMessagesMetadata
properties.type: (msg: BaseMessage<>, idx: number) => MessageMetadata<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined
properties.description: Get metadata for a specific message by index.

properties.name: getSubagent
properties.description: Get a subagent stream by the tool call ID that spawned it.

properties.name: getSubagentsByMessage
properties.description: Get subagent streams spawned by the tool calls on a specific AI message.

properties.name: getSubagentsByType
properties.description: Get subagent streams by their configured subagent type/name.

properties.name: getToolCalls
properties.description: Get tool call results associated with an AI message (LangGraph types).

properties.name: hasValue
properties.description: True once at least one value or message has been received.

properties.name: isThreadLoading
properties.description: True while a thread switch is loading state from the server.

properties.name: joinStream
properties.description: Join an already-running stream by run ID.

properties.name: langGraphHistory
properties.description: Raw LangGraph history (ThreadState[]). Use `history` for AgentCheckpoint[].

properties.name: langGraphInterrupts
properties.description: All interrupts received during the current run (raw LangGraph shape).

properties.name: langGraphMessages
properties.description: Raw LangChain BaseMessage list. Use `messages` for chat rendering.

properties.name: langGraphToolCalls
properties.description: Raw LangGraph tool calls (with run-state). Use `toolCalls` for chat rendering.

properties.name: lifecycle
properties.description:
Lifecycle signals for observability/telemetry. Eight read-only signals
capture key transitions (first stream chunk, first interrupt, tool
call start/complete, thread create/persist, errors). All reset on
`switchThread()`. See AgentLifecycle.

properties.name: messageCheckpoints
properties.description:
Optional reactive map of `messageId → checkpointId`, computed by
walking history once: for each checkpoint, find the most recent
assistant message present in its `values.messages` and pair them.
UIs use this to anchor inline checkpoint markers on each assistant
turn. Missing on adapters that don't compute it.

properties.name: queue
properties.description: Pending server-side runs created via `multitaskStrategy: 'enqueue'`.

properties.name: regenerate
properties.description:
Discards the assistant message at the given index AND all messages after
it, then re-runs the agent against the trimmed conversation tail. The
preceding user message (at index - 1) is preserved and re-submitted as
the agent's input. No new user message is added to the history.

Throws if the message at `index` is not 'assistant' role, or if the
agent is currently loading another response.

properties.name: reload
properties.description: Re-submit the last input to restart the stream.

properties.name: setBranch
properties.description: Set the active branch for time-travel navigation.

properties.name: submit
properties.type: (input: AgentSubmitInput | null | undefined, opts: AgentSubmitOptions & LangGraphSubmitOptions) => Promise<void>
properties.description: Submit input, resume commands, checkpoint forks, or other LangGraph run options.

properties.name: switchThread
properties.description: Switch to a different thread, resetting derived state.

properties.name: toolProgress
properties.description: Progress updates for currently executing tools.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Current agent state values (raw, typed per the type parameter T).

name: StreamEvent
description: An event emitted by a LangGraph stream.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Event type identifier (e.g., 'values', 'messages', 'error', 'interrupt').

name: SubagentStreamRef
description: Reference to a subagent's streaming state.

properties.name: messages
properties.description: Messages from the subagent conversation.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Optional human-readable subagent type/name.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Current execution status of the subagent.

properties.name: toolCallId
properties.description: The tool call ID that spawned this subagent.

properties.name: values
properties.description: Current state values from the subagent.

properties.name: durability
properties.description:
Whether to checkpoint during the run (or only at the end/interruption).
- `"async"`: Save checkpoint asynchronously while the next step executes (default).
- `"sync"`: Save checkpoint synchronously before the next step starts.
- `"exit"`: Save checkpoint only when the graph exits.

properties.name: onError
properties.description:
Callback that is called when an error occurs during this specific submit call.
Unlike the hook-level `onError`, this allows handling errors on a per-submit basis,
e.g. to show a retry button or a specific error message to the user.

properties.name: streamResumable
properties.description:
Mark the stream as resumable. All events emitted during the run will be temporarily persisted
in order to be re-emitted if the stream is re-joined.

properties.name: streamSubgraphs
properties.description:
Whether or not to stream the nodes of any subgraphs called
by the assistant.

properties.name: threadId
properties.description:
The ID to use when creating a new thread. When provided, this ID will be used
for thread creation when threadId is `null` or `undefined`.
This enables optimistic UI updates where you know the thread ID
before the thread is actually created.

properties.name: checkpoint
properties.description: Checkpoint of the thread state

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: Time of state creation

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Metadata for this state

properties.name: next
properties.description: The next nodes to execute. If empty, the thread is done until new input is received

properties.name: parent_checkpoint
properties.description: The parent checkpoint. If missing, this is the root checkpoint

properties.name: tasks
properties.description: Tasks to execute in this step. If already attempted, may contain an error

properties.name: values
properties.description: The state values

name: BagTemplate
description: Template for the bag type.

name: InferBag
description:
Infer the Bag type from an agent, defaulting to the provided Bag.

Currently returns the provided Bag for all types.
Can be extended in the future to extract Bag from agent types.

name: LangGraphMultitaskStrategy
description: Strategy for handling concurrent LangGraph runs on the same thread.

name: ResourceStatus
description:
Runtime constant mirroring Angular's ResourceStatus string-union type.
Angular 21 ships ResourceStatus as a pure string-union type (no runtime value),
so we provide a const-object shim for code that needs runtime comparisons.

name: LANGGRAPH_CLIENT
description:
Optional adapter clients can pass an explicit Client (e.g. for
 testing). When omitted, the adapter constructs one via
 createLangGraphClient.

name: LANGGRAPH_CLIENT_OPTIONS
description:
App-wide LangGraph SDK client tuning (e.g. `maxRetries`). Provide once at the
app root; both the agent's default FetchStreamTransport and the
LangGraphThreadsAdapter read it so the retry budget is configured in
one place. A call-site `agent({ clientOptions })` or per-agent
`provideAgent({ clientOptions })` overrides it for that agent.
Absent → the SDK default.

name: ResourceStatus
description:
Runtime constant mirroring Angular's ResourceStatus string-union type.
Angular 21 ships ResourceStatus as a pure string-union type (no runtime value),
so we provide a const-object shim for code that needs runtime comparisons.

name: createLangGraphClient
description:
Construct a LangGraph SDK Client that accepts both absolute URLs
(`http://localhost:2024`) and relative `/api`-style paths that get
proxied by middleware in production. The SDK itself rejects
relative URLs, so this helper rewrites them against
`window.location.origin` when running in the browser.

Single source of truth for the absolute-URL rewrite — the streaming
transport (`fetch-stream.transport.ts`) and the threads adapter
(`LangGraphThreadsAdapter`) both go through here.

`clientOptions.maxRetries` maps to the SDK's `callerOptions.maxRetries`,
which governs how many times a failed request (including the initial
stream connect) is retried with exponential backoff before the error
surfaces. Omitted → the SDK default (currently 4). Apps under test set
`0` so a forced connection failure surfaces immediately instead of after
the full backoff window.

name: injectAgent
description:
Retrieve the LangGraph-backed Agent from the current Angular injection context.

Mirrors `@threadplane/ag-ui`'s `injectAgent()` so consumer code is identical
regardless of which adapter is wired in `app.config.ts`. The agent is a
singleton scoped to the injector that called `provideAgent()` — re-provide
in a child component's `providers: []` to scope a different agent to that
subtree (Angular's hierarchical DI handles the rest).

**Typed state via AgentRef.** Pass the same ref that was supplied to
`provideAgent(ref, …)` to carry the state type through DI without repeating
the generic at every call site:

```ts
const agent = injectAgent(TRIP); // LangGraphAgent<TripState>
```

The no-arg form defaults to `LangGraphAgent<Record<string, unknown>>`.

name: mockLangGraphAgent
description:
Creates a mock LangGraphAgent with writable signals for testing.
Control state by writing to the returned writable signals directly.

Neutral `Agent`-contract signals come from mockAgent; LangGraph-specific
signals are declared here and layered on top.
signature: mockLangGraphAgent(initial: MockAgentOptions & { hasValue?: boolean; isThreadLoading?: boolean; langGraphMessages?: BaseMessage<MessageStructure<MessageToolSet>, MessageType>[] }): MockLangGraphAgent<>

params.name: initial
params.type: MockAgentOptions & { hasValue?: boolean; isThreadLoading?: boolean; langGraphMessages?: BaseMessage<MessageStructure<MessageToolSet>, MessageType>[] }

name: provideAgent
signature: provideAgent(ref: AgentRef<T>, configOrFactory: AgentConfig<T, BagTemplate> | () => AgentConfig<T>): Provider[]

name: provideFakeAgent
description:
Wire an in-process fake LangGraph agent into Angular DI.

Streams a canned assistant reply (see FakeAgentConfig) with no backend —
the symmetric counterpart to @threadplane/ag-ui's provideFakeAgent(). For
advanced manual scripting (tool calls, interrupts, multi-batch), provide
the agent yourself with
`provideAgent({ assistantId, transport: new MockAgentTransport(...) })`.

name: refreshOnRunEnd
description:
Call `fn` whenever the agent's status transitions out of `'running'`
(i.e. when a run completes — success, error, or interrupt). Useful
for refreshing thread lists, telemetry, or any other state that
lags the agent.

Must be called within an injection context (constructor or
`runInInjectionContext`) — uses Angular's `effect` under the hood.
signature: refreshOnRunEnd(agent: LangGraphAgent<>, fn: () => void | Promise<void>): void

name: refreshOnTransition
description:
Call `fn` whenever any of the watched signals transitions from a
truthy "active" value to a non-active value. Generic version of
refreshOnRunEnd for callers tracking custom state machines.

Must be called within an injection context.
signature: refreshOnTransition(watch: Signal<T>, isActive: (v: T) => boolean, fn: () => void | Promise<void>): void

name: toAbsoluteApiUrl
description:
Exported separately so non-Client callers (e.g. raw fetch) can
 share the same normalization logic.
